commit.message:
feat(ios): Add icon picker, battery quantity, and AI suggestion to Add Device Type (#978)

Enrich the iOS Add Device Type screen to match the Compose implementation:

- Icon picker grid: 4-column LazyVGrid with SF Symbol icons, selection
  highlighting with primary color circle, sorted with used icons first
- Battery quantity controls: HStack with minus/plus buttons and integer
  display (min 1), wired to the ViewModel wrapper state
- AI icon suggestion: ViewModel wrapper subscribes to suggestedIcon from
  KMP uiState, auto-selects suggested icon in grid, shows spinner on
  name field while suggesting
- ViewModelWrapper updates: Expose selectedIcon, batteryQuantity,
  isSuggestingIcon, suggestedIcon, and usedIcons from KMP ViewModel
- SFSymbolMapper: Add availableIcons list and displayName helper
- Localization: Add new string keys for icon section, field labels,
  and battery quantity controls
- Snapshot tests: Updated to match new ContentView signature

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign About Us section, standardize CTAs, update carousels

- Add new AboutUs component with split layout, animated image frame,
  parallax tilt entrance, and cross-hatch texture on light blue-gray bg
- Add counting stat animations to WhyUs section with icons and staggered load
- Standardize CTA font sizes across all sections (text-xl / text-base)
- Fix ShimmerButton centering (inline-flex) and remove hardcoded span sizes
- Increase Hero CTA button size
- Remove 11 images from carousels and public folder
- Swap carousel scroll directions (L-R, R-L, L-R)
- Remove unused deployment.md and instructions.md

Add RoadRunner fallback for models COPASI can't integrate

parameters. When COPASI produces NaN results (e.g. stiff models like
MODEL1209260000), it automatically falls back to tellurium/RoadRunner.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Use fps=24 in Remotion to match LTX 2.3 natively

Replace ffmpeg re-encode step with fps={24} in Remotion composition.
LTX 2.3 outputs 24fps — setting Remotion fps to match eliminates the
mismatch without any quality loss. Also fix shiftLyricsTiming import
(must come from utils/lyricsParser, not LyricsOverlay).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add project sphere selector with industry-specific defaults

Adds a prominent radio-button sphere selector at the top of the Inputs
tab. Switching sphere instantly repopulates all input fields with
calibrated defaults for that industry.

Spheres and key characteristics:
- 🏭 Production  — 5yr, 12% growth, 35% COGS, WACC 10%
- 🛣️ Toll Road   — 15yr, 4% growth (inflation-linked), DSO=3, heavy debt
- 🌾 Agriculture — 10yr, DIO=120 (harvest cycle), lower 15% tax
- ⛏️ Mining      — 12yr, sustaining capex model, WACC 11%
- 🚢 Port        — 20yr, 10% COGS, infrastructure WACC 6%

All five spheres produce positive NPV, real IRR, and balanced BS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Normalize LTX 2.3 clips from 24fps to 25fps before Remotion merge

LTX 2.3 generates 24fps video but Remotion renders at 25fps. This frame
mismatch causes choppy playback in the final output. Added mandatory Step 5.5
to re-encode all clips to 25fps before concatenation.

commit.message:
Initial commit: DCF Financial Model Dash app (OOP)

Full interactive DCF model built with Plotly Dash and Bootstrap.
- 11 OOP financial model classes (Revenues, OpEx, FA, WC, Taxes,
  Debt, Equity, P&L, CashFlow, BalanceSheet, Evaluation)
- DCFModel orchestrator runs all blocks in dependency order
- 12-tab Dash UI: inputs panel + one results tab per financial block
- Charts: waterfall P&L, grouped CF bars, heatmap sensitivity,
  IRR gauge, stacked BS, and more
- Balance sheet guaranteed to balance by construction (Years 1-N)
- KPI cards: NPV, IRR, PBP, DPBP with color-coded indicators

fix: onnxruntime GPU check — print() result so grep sees it, use $PYTHON_CMD

get_device() returns a string but wasn't printed, so grep -q GPU always
failed and the reinstall triggered unconditionally. Also replaced bare
`python` with `$PYTHON_CMD` in vastai_setup.sh for consistency.

commit.message:
Add descriptive statistics, model selection, and multicollinearity sections

Based on re-reading all 6 papers (especially Rhee AMJ 2024), add:
- Descriptive statistics section in Results with proper standard: intro
  table + core variable interpretation + VIF + centering (3-6 sentences,
  matching actual top-journal practice rather than over-engineering)
- Statistical Method section with model choice justification pattern
  (DV properties → diagnostic tests → multiple reasons), multicollinearity
  handling (VIF reporting, mean-centering, separate analysis for correlated
  variables), standard error clustering, and lag structure with theoretical
  justification
- Updated Phase 1 diagnostics to check for VIF reporting and model
  selection justification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: rename missions to observatories, add MCP logging and HTTP improvements

- Rename data/missions/ → data/observatories/ throughout codebase

- Rename all internal APIs: load_mission_json → load_observatory_json, etc.

- Make MCP tool functions async for proper event loop access
- Add HTTP 5xx retry (was only retrying timeouts/connection errors)

- Replace print() with logging in build_catalog.py library functions

commit.message:
Add shuffle_over_same_T_seed parameter for randomizing genome insertion order (#359)

* Add shuffle_same_T_groups_seed option to shuffle rows within same-T groups

After sorting by dstream_T but before exploding, optionally shuffle rows
within same-dstream_T groups using a polars window function. This randomizes
insertion order of genomes with equal generation counts. The option is
exposed through both the Python API and CLI for surface_unpack_reconstruct
and surface_build_tree.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017kYcpseL6jMFYf9kBGDXg9

* Add smoke tests and rename to shuffle_over_same_T_seed

- Add API smoke tests for surface_unpack_reconstruct and
  surface_build_tree with shuffle_over_same_T_seed parameter
- Add CLI smoke tests for --shuffle-over-same-T-seed flag
- Rename parameter from shuffle_same_T_groups_seed to
  shuffle_over_same_T_seed per review feedback
- Fix black formatting issues

https://claude.ai/code/session_017kYcpseL6jMFYf9kBGDXg9

* Set dstream_T sorted hint after sort, avoid lazy conversion in shuffle

Move set_sorted() call to right after the sort and keep shuffle
operating on the eager DataFrame directly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017kYcpseL6jMFYf9kBGDXg9

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove CPUTime dependency to fix Windows heap corruption crash

CPUTime.jl's CPUtime_us() function allocates a buffer for uv_rusage_t
using a hardcoded size calculation based on Clong (4 bytes on Windows).
Julia 1.12+ ships libuv 2.0.0-dev which changed uv_timeval_t to use
int64_t (8 bytes) for tv_sec instead of long, making the actual struct
16 bytes larger than the allocated buffer. This causes uv_getrusage to
write past the buffer, resulting in STATUS_HEAP_CORRUPTION (0xC0000374)
on Windows x64.

Replace all CPUTime usage with Julia's built-in time() function, which
is sufficient for the wall-clock timing needs here.

Co-Authored-By: Chris Rackauckas <accounts@chrisrackauckas.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
